message:
fix(server): UTF-8 safe timestamp fraction parsing and dashboard stats dedup

The TUI poller's parse_text_timestamp previously sliced frac_str with
byte indices, which could panic on multi-byte UTF-8 input. This
replaces the byte slice with chars().take(6).collect() to safely
handle any input.

The dashboard screen had a fallthrough else branch that unconditionally
overwrote current_db_stats with any incoming stats, even if they had an
older timestamp. This caused flickering metrics when out-of-order stats
arrived. Removing the else branch ensures stats are only updated when
the incoming timestamp is strictly newer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
